title: The Question
subtitle: How Rocky Mount Was Chosen
description: Follow the correspondence that led Washington to choose this remote frontier site as the seat of government for the Southwest Territory, serving from 1790 until the government moved to Knoxville in 1792.

documents.title: "Where ought the Governor to reside?"
documents.excerpt: Washington asks Knox the crucial question about locating the territorial government.

documents.title: Knox Recommends Holston
documents.excerpt: Knox recommends the Holston region, noting Cobb's house as "a respectable residence."

documents.title: Blount's Commission
documents.excerpt: Washington appoints Blount Governor and Superintendent of Indian Affairs.

documents.title: "Glass Windows, Fireplace, etc."
documents.excerpt: Blount announces his arrival at Rocky Mount, impressed by its frontier refinement.

title: The Treaty
subtitle: Making Peace with the Cherokee
description: The Treaty of Holston promised perpetual peace. Follow the documents from negotiation through ratification.

documents.title: Planning the Treaty
documents.excerpt: Blount reports on preparations for the treaty conference.

documents.title: Treaty of Holston
documents.excerpt: Forty-two Cherokee leaders sign a treaty establishing boundaries and "perpetual peace."

documents.title: Washington's Proclamation
documents.excerpt: The President formally ratifies the treaty, making it the law of the land.

documents.title: Additional Article
documents.excerpt: Cherokee representatives negotiate increased compensation in Philadelphia.

title: The Signers
subtitle: 42 Cherokee Leaders
description: Each mark on the treaty represents a person who shaped history. Meet the Cherokee leaders who signed.

documents.title: Treaty Signatories
documents.excerpt: Hanging Maw, Bloody Fellow, John Watts, Doublehead, and 38 others put their marks.

title: The Governor
subtitle: Blount's Life at Rocky Mount
description: William Blount governed from this cabin for fourteen months. His letters reveal the daily reality of frontier administration.

documents.title: Williamson's Recommendation
documents.excerpt: Hugh Williamson recommends Blount as someone who "commands such general respect."

documents.title: First Days at Rocky Mount
documents.excerpt: Blount settles in, noting the comfort of his accommodations on the frontier.

documents.title: Rocky Mount Property
documents.excerpt: The only detailed description of Rocky Mount's buildings during Blount's tenure.

documents.title: Final Months
documents.excerpt: Blount reports from Rocky Mount as plans form to relocate to Knoxville.

title: The Press
subtitle: First Tennessee News
description: The Knoxville Gazette was Tennessee's first newspaper. Follow frontier news from 1791 to statehood.

documents.title: First Issue
documents.excerpt: George Roulstone prints Tennessee's first newspaper in the new capital.

documents.title: Second Issue
documents.excerpt: News of the territory continues with the second published edition.

documents.title: Mid-Year Report
documents.excerpt: Frontier news and notices from the territorial capital.

documents.title: Statehood Approaches
documents.excerpt: Tennessee prepares to join the Union as the sixteenth state.
